context.description: German philosophical nonfiction page arguing that conscious self-change cannot fully reproduce archetypal symbolic structures without a complete understanding of their laws. It proposes a higher consciousness state, compared with an Über-Ich, as the source of archetypes and broader reality models. The discussion links cognitive blindness, social conflict, and speculative evolutionary limits.
normalization_notes: Derived title from source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: Conscious self-change requires a full grasp of symbolic laws that waking consciousness cannot reach.
Archetypes are presented as signs of a higher consciousness state, compared with the superego, from which hidden rules and laws become visible.
The page extends the argument toward new reality models, social conflict, and speculative evolutionary limits of human cognition.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2; page 90; German nonfiction philosophy on archetypes, consciousness, symbolic laws, higher awareness, and reality models.
